Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 4, 158 [SB Barutu dub ha-la][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x u 30 x

[...] ... and 30 ...

r 2'2'

[...] x NE 12 ⸢U₄⸣ [...] x 7 a-na TUN₃ u MUR i-ši-ma

[...] ... 12 days [...] .... Multiply 7 by the stomach and the lungs and

r 3'3'

[...] šum₄-ma 7 u₃ 3.30 i-na [...] u₃ NIG₂.KA₉mi lu-mur

[...] If 7 and 3;30 from [...] "Let me see the calculation and [...]".

r 4'4'

[...] 7 gišTUKUL 7ti ša₂ dUTU GISKIM UDU.NITA₂ ne₂-re-bu ša₂ NAM.AZU ina ṣa-a-ti u NIG₂.KA₉ i-tap-pal

[...] 7, the seven Weapons of Šamaš, ominous sign of the ram, replaces the entrance of extispicy in the commentary and calculation.

r 5'5'

[...] x lu-mur u₂U e-še-ret bu-urBUR₃ pi-il-šu ši-lu₄ ša₂ UZU

Let me see [...] .... (The sign) U (with the reading) u (means) "ten". (The sign) U (with the reading) bur₃ (means) "perforation" (or) "hole" of the flesh.

r 6'6'

[...] x pe-tu-u₂ : pa-ṭa-ru BUR₃ a-na DU₈ i-ši-ma 10 A.RA₂ 30 : 5 : 5 : BUR₃ 5ti MU-NI

[...] .... To open (means): to loosen. Multiply the Perforation by the Fissure and 10 times 0;30 (is): 5. : 5 : Its name is "five Perforations".

r 7'7'

[... x]-GAL₂.BI 6 6 ana DU₈ i-ši-ma 6 A.RA₂ 30 : 3 : 3 DU₈ 3ti MU-NI

[... n]th part is 6. Multiply 6 by the Fissure and 6 times 0;30 (is): 3. : 3. Its name is "three Perforations".

r 8'8'

[...] x gišTUKUL ša₂ BUR₃ u₃ DU₈ ib-nu-u₂ 4 u₃ 5ti 3 DU₈ 3ti a-na IGIi ša₂ UDU.NITA₂ i-ši-⸢ma⸣

[...] ... the Weapon that the Perforation and the Fissure created (are) 4 and five. Multiply 3, the three Perforations, by the front of the ram and

r 9'9'

[...] x u MANu₂ MU-NI 1.20 : NU NUu : NU : u₂-ba-nu GISKIM 1at ša₂ ŠU.SI gišTUKUL BUR₃ u₃ [...]  ; NIG₂.KA₉-MEŠ-šu₂-nu

[...] and its second name is 1.20. NU (has reading) nû. NU (means) Finger, one sign of the Finger. Weapon, Perforation, and [...] are their calculations.

r 10'10'

[...] U₄.DU₁₁.GA ša₂-ka-nam ir-riš-ka ša₂ ŠU.SI NIG₂.KA₉-šu₂ ḫi-ṭi <NIG₂>.KA₉sa ku-mur-ma 55 [...]

[...] requests you to determine the [...] of the time limit. Check the calculation of the Finger. Sum the calculation and (it is) 55. [...]

r 11'11'

[... x]-GAL₂.BI ku-mur-ma 6 BUR₃ tam-mar IGI 9 6.40 IGI-<<10>>-GAL₂.BI 9 6.40 ud-da-zal-le-e [...]

Sum [... n]th part and you will see 6, the Perforation. The reciprocal of 9 is 6;40. Its reciprocal is 9, The uddazallû-date of [...] is 6;40.

r 12'12'

[...] x-mi reš-ka li-kil šum₄-ma LUGAL EN-ka a-na ITI U₄.DU₁₁.[GA ša₂]-ka-na ir-[riš-ka]

[...] let your head hold .... If the king your lord requests you to determine the month of the time limit

r 13'13'

[... ud-da-zal]-⸢le⸣-e u₄-mu a-na 30 U₄-MEŠ ITI i-ši-ma 6.40!(50) A.RA₂ 30!(40) 3.20 3.20 ud-da-zal-le-e [...]

[...]. Multiply [...], the uddazallû-date of a day, by 30, the days of a month, and 6;40 times 30 (is) 3 20. The uddazallû-date of [...] is 3 20.

r 14'14'

[... U₄.DU₁₁].GA ša₂-ka-nam DU₈ U₄.DU₁₁.GA i-na ŠIDtu₂ ŠU.SI IGImar 3 A.RA₂ 3 9 [...]

[...] to determine the [...] of the time limit. You will see the solution of the time limit from the calculation of the Finger. 3 time 3 (is) 9. [...]

(catchline)
r 15'15'

[...] ⸢ŠU⸣.SI 1 DU₈ 3.20 a-da-an-šu₂ a-na 3.20 IRI DABbat ANu₂ SURun [...]

[...] the Finger (has) 1 Fissure: its time limit is 3 20. In 3 20(days) the city will be seized, rain will fall, [...].

(1 line blank)
(colophon)
r 16'16'

[DUB ḪA].LA la₃ AL.TIL GIM SUMUN-šu₂ SAR-ma ba-ru₃ IM mBAša₂-a bu₁₂-kur₂ mdINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL m⸢E₂⸣.[KUR—za-kir ...]

[DUB] HA.LA, not finished. Copied and checked according to its original. Tablet of Iqiša, son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir, [...].

(1 line blank)
r 17'17'

[...] ⸢pa-liḫ⸣ dIŠKUR u DINGIR-šu₂ la₃ TUM₃-šu₂ ina me-reš-ti-šu₂ la u₂-⸢šam-qiš-šu₂⸣ [...]

[...]. Whoever reveres Adad and his (personal) god shall not take it away, shall not intentionally let it fall. [...]
